description: Phase 5.3 error handling strategies: per-component error_types / detection / response / surfacing classified with source-attested error names.

template_variables.active_constraints:
1. The Governed Stream is lossless. All execution trace content — reasoning steps, self-corrections, tool call invocations, tool results — is captured in full regardless of what subset is used for any given downstream purpose. (Authority 7, source: 55a61a95-64fd-475b-a985-7962936fb07e)
2. No governing constraint may be truncated silently. Governing constraints (Authority Level 6+) are always delivered in full via the stdin directive channel. (Authority 7, source: b680da1d-f6d6-4113-ab4d-1a166154aff7)
3. Prompt Templates use namespace prefixing ([JC:] and [P:]) and separate context scopes at all times. (Authority 7, source: a67d4502-972e-4c08-b482-19fbc402a5e9)
4. All Artifacts are owned by JanumiCode and stored in the Governed Stream database. (Authority 7, source: a1030128-7334-4d7a-8ae9-8900de3c2993)
5. The Governed Stream is single-threaded. No parallel Workflow Runs in a Workspace. (Authority 7, source: 5c77f777-a698-405b-9881-b6813ae04ec5)
6. Agents never exercise judgment. Judgment is always escalated to the human. (Authority 7, source: b3e3ec17-574d-4915-99a2-244e0e71639e)
7. Every human interaction is recorded in the Governed Stream in full detail. (Authority 7, source: d7f5ea89-7265-4416-847f-348290ea28a2)
8. Every Phase Gate requires human approval. No automated gate passage. (Authority 7, source: ba84c364-c2f2-4a63-86ff-f3a49aba6c19)
9. Every phase is mandatory and executed in order. The Orchestrator cannot skip phases. (Authority 7, source: 0450a160-f9ec-4e41-ab94-188f37bf1794)
10. 100% correctness and completeness — always. All three layers of correctness (Section 1.6) are required. (Authority 7, source: 4394e1a8-f6d1-42c0-a25c-7991e6d02a5e)
template_variables.system_requirements_summary:
17 System Requirements:
  SR-001 [critical]: System shall perform successful mapping lookup for valid slug, returning encrypted payload metadata and target destination. (traces to: ["FR-002-001.1"])
  SR-002 [critical]: System shall validate and reject malicious or invalid requests to prevent processing. (traces to: ["US-001-C1-1"])
  SR-003 [high]: System shall monitor redirect endpoint latency P95 via Prometheus histograms under nominal load to maintain latency ≤ 100 ms within rolling 5-minute windows. (traces to: ["NFR-001","NFR-001-D-001"])
  SR-004 [high]: System shall verify monthly uptime for redirect endpoint including planned maintenance via synthetic probes to ensure verified uptime ≥ 99.9% over rolling 30-day windows. (traces to: ["NFR-002","NFR-AVAIL-002-D-001"])
  SR-005 [high]: System shall achieve Recovery Time Objective (RTO) on full regional failure ≤ 15 minutes. (traces to: ["NFR-003"])
  SR-006 [high]: System shall store original URLs encrypted at rest using AES-256 cipher suite with zero plaintext occurrence in direct reads. (traces to: ["NFR-004"])
  SR-007 [high]: System shall emit structured JSON logs to stdout for ingestion by platform, retaining audit logs ≥ 365 days and alerting on ingestion failure < 1 minute. (traces to: ["NFR-005"])
  SR-008 [high]: System shall enforce HTTPS on all public endpoints with HSTS headers and redirect HTTP requests. (traces to: ["NFR-006"])
  SR-009 [high]: System shall ensure every stored slug in the mappings table is unique with no duplicates. (traces to: ["NFR-007"])
  SR-010 [high]: System shall purge valid deletion requests from Sharers from persistent storage and ephemeral cache within 24 hours of receipt. (traces to: ["NFR-008"])
  SR-011 [high]: System shall export 100% of stored link data for a requesting user for GDPR Right to Access requests with zero data loss. (traces to: ["NFR-009"])
  SR-012 [high]: System shall execute daily automated backups and replicate with zero integrity errors to ensure 100% job success. (traces to: ["NFR-010"])
  SR-013 [high]: System shall block threat indicators with latency ≤ 5 seconds and update threat feeds ≤ 15 minutes. (traces to: ["NFR-011"])
  SR-014 [high]: System shall enforce rate limit of 1000 requests per IP per rolling 60-second window and reject excess with HTTP 429. (traces to: ["NFR-012"])
  SR-015 [high]: System shall block malicious URL submissions before storage in content repository. (traces to: ["NFR-013"])
  SR-016 [high]: System shall generate notification attempts for every policy-expiry event before the expiry timestamp with zero missed notifications. (traces to: ["NFR-014"])
  SR-017 [high]: System shall handle timeout conditions without triggering service crashes. (traces to: ["NFR-015"])
template_variables.component_model_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: TinyURL is a frictionless, public URL shortening service ensuring links are instantly accessible and reliably tracked without requiring user accounts or complex setup. It empowers Link Sharers, Clickers, and API Clients through a secure, minimal architecture focused on speed and simplicity.: TinyURL is a minimal HTTP service that converts long URLs into short, six-character slugs for public sharing and instantly redirects traffic back to the original destination. It operates without user authentication, focusing purely on the core value of link compression and reliable delivery. The system leverages domains like the URL Shortening Engine, Threat Detection Gateway, and Encrypted Storage Layer to ensure security and performance, while integrations manage rate limiting, disaster recovery, and logging.
Capabilities: [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]; [object Object]

[Backlog] comp-delete-purger (Tier D leaf under link-lifecycle-processor): DeletePurger
  - Purge valid deletion requests from Sharers from persistent storage and ephemeral cache.

[Backlog] comp-gdpr-exporter (Tier D leaf under link-lifecycle-processor): GdprExporter
  - Exports 100% of stored link data for a requesting user for GDPR Right to Access requests.

[Backlog] comp-link-mapping-repository (Tier D leaf under link-mapping-repository): LinkMappingRepository
  - Performs successful mapping lookup for valid slug, returning encrypted payload metadata and target destination.
  - Stores original URLs encrypted at rest using AES-256 cipher suite with zero plaintext occurrence in direct reads.
  - Ensures every stored slug in the mappings table is unique with no duplicates.

[Backlog] comp-platform-audit (Tier D leaf under platform-audit): ReliabilityAudit
  - Emits structured JSON logs to stdout for ingestion by platform, retaining audit logs for a minimum of 365 days.

[Backlog] comp-backup-replication-engine (Tier D leaf under platform-disaster-recovery): BackupReplicationEngine
  - Execute daily automated backups and replicate with zero integrity errors to disaster recovery storage.
  - Verify backup checksums before committing replication tasks to ensure data integrity.

[Backlog] comp-failure-detection (Tier D leaf under platform-disaster-recovery): Failure Detection Handler
  - Ingest events from platform-fault-tolerance to identify regional failure conditions.
  - Raise recovery-trigger event upon detecting RTO-impacting failure state.

[Backlog] comp-recovery-planner (Tier D leaf under platform-disaster-recovery): Recovery Planner Module
  - Resolve region topology from link-mapping-repository to identify required endpoints.
  - Generate state-restoration sequence plan targeting specific regional endpoints.

[Backlog] comp-request-timeout-enforcer (Tier D leaf under platform-fault-tolerance): Request Timeout Enforcer
  - Intercept incoming requests and enforce configured timeout limits.
  - Terminate long-running requests safely without dropping connections abruptly.

[Backlog] graceful-shutdown-coordinator (Tier D leaf under platform-fault-tolerance): Graceful Shutdown Coordinator
  - Manage signal listeners to initiate resource cleanup before process termination.
  - Ensure no new requests are accepted during the drain window.

[Backlog] comp-rel-latency-monitor (Tier D leaf under platform-observability): ReliabilityLatencyMonitor
  - Monitor redirect endpoint latency P95 via Prometheus histograms to ensure latency remains within 100 ms.

[Backlog] comp-rel-uptime-verifier (Tier D leaf under platform-observability): ReliabilityUptimeVerifier
  - Verify monthly uptime for redirect endpoint via synthetic probes to ensure verified uptime ≥ 99.9%.

[Backlog] comp-sec-request-validation (Tier D leaf under sec-validation-gateway): RequestValidationEngine
  - Validate and reject malicious or invalid requests to prevent processing.
  - Block malicious URL submissions before storage in content repository.

[Backlog] comp-sec-transport-enforcer (Tier D leaf under sec-validation-gateway): TransportSecurityEnforcer
  - Enforce HTTPS transport layer, inject HSTS headers, and redirect HTTP requests to HTTPS.
  - Validate incoming requests originate from SecureChannel.
template_variables.api_definitions_summary:
Component comp-link-mapping-repository:
  POST /api/v1/shorten (auth: none)
  GET /api/v1/redirect/{slug} (auth: none)
  GET /api/v1/meta/{slug} (auth: bearer)
Component comp-gdpr-exporter:
  POST /api/v1/gdpr/export (auth: bearer)
Component comp-delete-purger:
  POST /api/v1/admin/purge (auth: bearer)
Component comp-backup-replication-engine:
  POST /api/v1/dr/backup (auth: bearer)
  GET /api/v1/dr/status (auth: bearer)
Component comp-platform-audit:
  GET /api/v1/audit/health (auth: none)
Component comp-failure-detection:
  POST /api/v1/fault/report (auth: none)
Component comp-recovery-planner:
  GET /api/v1/recovery/plan (auth: bearer)
Component comp-request-timeout-enforcer:
  GET /api/v1/middleware/health (auth: none)
Component comp-rel-latency-monitor:
  GET /api/v1/metrics/latency (auth: none)
Component comp-rel-uptime-verifier:
  GET /api/v1/health/uptime (auth: none)
Component comp-sec-request-validation:
  GET /api/v1/waf/status (auth: none)
Component comp-sec-transport-enforcer:
  GET /api/v1/transport/config (auth: none)
template_variables.technical_constraints_summary:
TECH-POSTGRES-16 — Postgres — database — Postgres 16+ on a single managed instance is required for persistence.
TECH-HTTPS-ONLY — HTTPS — integration_protocol — HTTPS only on all public endpoints, with HTTP requests redirecting to HTTPS.
TECH-CONTAINERIZED-SERVICE — backend — A single containerised service is required; no microservices.
TECH-JSON-LOGS-STDOUT — monitoring — Structured JSON logs to stdout are required; logs are ingested by the platform standard log aggregator.
TECH-AES-256-ENCRYPTION-AT-REST — AES-256 — security — Original URLs stored in the mappings table must be encrypted at rest using AES-256.

{
  "strategies": [
    {
      "component_id": "comp-link-mapping-repository",
      "error_types": [
        "validation_error",
        "authorization_error",
        "http_400_bad_request",
        "http_404_not_found",
        "http_500_internal_server_error",
        "http_503_service_unavailable",
        "unique_constraint_violation"
      ],
      "detection": "Validate slug against schema and unique index on mappings table. Detect duplicate slug attempts via database unique constraint violations. Intercept requests failing schema validation at middleware.",
      "response": "Return 400 Bad Request for invalid schema. Return 409 Conflict for duplicate slug. Return 404 Not Found for missing slug. On 500, propagate exception. Retry database read on connection pool exhaustion.",
      "surfacing": [
        "HTTP Status Code",
        "JSON Error Payload",
        "Structured Log to stdout"
      ]
    },
    {
      "component_id": "comp-gdpr-exporter",
      "error_types": [
        "http_401_unauthorized",
        "http_403_forbidden",
        "http_500_internal_server_error",
        "data_integrity_error"
      ],
      "detection": "Verify bearer token scope for GDPR export. Check storage for user data existence. Verify export pipeline integrity after data retrieval.",
      "response": "Return 401/403 on auth failure. Return 500 on export failure. Trigger audit log for incomplete exports. Retry export job within policy window.",
      "surfacing": [
        "HTTP Status Code",
        "Encrypted Payload",
        "Audit Log"
      ]
    },
    {
      "component_id": "comp-delete-purger",
      "error_types": [
        "http_401_unauthorized",
        "http_400_bad_request",
        "http_500_internal_server_error",
        "storage_cleanup_failure"
      ],
      "detection": "Validate admin bearer token. Verify purge request validity against cache metadata. Check persistent storage delete status before expiration window.",
      "response": "Confirm deletion if valid. Return error if storage is locked or read-only. Flag deletion request for manual review if purge fails within 24 hours.",
      "surfacing": [
        "HTTP Status Code",
        "Admin Audit Log",
        "Retention Log"
      ]
    },
    {
      "component_id": "comp-backup-replication-engine",
      "error_types": [
        "http_500_internal_server_error",
        "http_503_service_unavailable",
        "integrity_error",
        "http_401_unauthorized"
      ],
      "detection": "Verify backup checksums before replication. Monitor replication job status endpoint. Check storage connectivity via health probes.",
      "response": "Abort replication on checksum mismatch. Retry with exponential backoff on transient network failures. Alert on repeated integrity errors.",
      "surfacing": [
        "Health Endpoint Response",
        "DR Status Log",
        "Alerting System"
      ]
    },
    {
      "component_id": "comp-platform-audit",
      "error_types": [
        "http_500_internal_server_error",
        "connection_refused",
        "disk_space_exhausted"
      ],
      "detection": "Monitor stdout buffer memory usage. Verify ingestion pipeline status. Track log write latency.",
      "response": "Flush logs if buffer full. Alert on ingestion failure < 1 minute. Continue non-blocking writes to avoid request drop.",
      "surfacing": [
        "JSON Log to stdout",
        "Audit Ingestion Metric",
        "Alert Channel"
      ]
    },
    {
      "component_id": "comp-failure-detection",
      "error_types": [
        "http_503_service_unavailable",
        "http_502_bad_gateway",
        "internal_server_error"
      ],
      "detection": "Ingest events from platform-fault-tolerance. Monitor synthetic probe failures. Detect regional heartbeat loss.",
      "response": "Raise recovery-trigger event. Isolate failed region. Generate state-restoration event without crashing the orchestrator.",
      "surfacing": [
        "Internal Event Bus",
        "Fault Report Log",
        "Recovery Trigger Signal"
      ]
    },
    {
      "component_id": "comp-recovery-planner",
      "error_types": [
        "http_502_bad_gateway",
        "http_503_service_unavailable",
        "topology_mismatch_error"
      ],
      "detection": "Resolve region topology from repository. Check endpoint availability in DR storage. Validate state-restoration sequence plan integrity.",

      "surfacing": [

        "System Status Log",
        "DR Plan Audit"
      ]
    },
    {
      "component_id": "comp-request-timeout-enforcer",
      "error_types": [
        "http_504_gateway_timeout",
        "http_503_service_unavailable",
        "connection_timeout"
      ],
      "detection": "Track request duration vs SLA. Monitor connection lifecycle. Detect slow query patterns.",

        "HTTP Timeout Response",
        "Request Timeout Log",
        "Middleware Health Endpoint"
      ]
    },
    {
      "component_id": "comp-graceful-shutdown-coordinator",
      "error_types": [
        "http_503_service_unavailable",

        "resource_cleanup_failure"

        "http_503_service_unavailable",
        "network_error",
        "probe_failure"
      ],
      "detection": "Execute synthetic probe. Check response time. Verify uptime threshold ≥ 99.9%.",
      "response": "Report downtime if threshold breached. Flag planned maintenance. Adjust uptime calculation for valid maintenance windows.",
      "surfacing": [
        "Health Endpoint Response",
        "Uptime Report Log",
        "Uptime Metric"
      ]
    },
    {
      "component_id": "comp-sec-request-validation",
      "error_types": [
        "http_400_bad_request",
        "http_403_forbidden",
        "http_429_too_many_requests",
        "malicious_content_blocked"
      ],
      "detection": "Check WAF rules. Scan URL for threats. Validate input schema. Verify threat feed latency ≤ 5 seconds.",
      "response": "Block request if malicious. Return 400 for invalid schema. Return 429 if rate limit exceeded. Log blocked attempt.",
      "surfacing": [
        "WAF Status Response",
        "Security Audit Log",
        "Threat Feed Log"
      ]
    },
    {
      "component_id": "comp-sec-transport-enforcer",
      "error_types": [
        "http_301_redirect",
        "http_500_internal_server_error",
        "ssl_certificate_error"
      ],
      "detection": "Check TLS certificate validity. Validate HSTS header presence. Verify SecureChannel origin.",
      "response": "Redirect HTTP requests to HTTPS. Inject HSTS headers. Terminate insecure connection.",
      "surfacing": [
        "Redirect Response",
        "Transport Config Endpoint Response",
        "SSL Status Log"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

strategies.detection: Validate slug against schema and unique index on mappings table. Detect duplicate slug attempts via database unique constraint violations. Intercept requests failing schema validation at middleware.
strategies.response: Return 400 Bad Request for invalid schema. Return 409 Conflict for duplicate slug. Return 404 Not Found for missing slug. On 500, propagate exception. Retry database read on connection pool exhaustion.

strategies.detection: Verify bearer token scope for GDPR export. Check storage for user data existence. Verify export pipeline integrity after data retrieval.
strategies.response: Return 401/403 on auth failure. Return 500 on export failure. Trigger audit log for incomplete exports. Retry export job within policy window.

strategies.detection: Validate admin bearer token. Verify purge request validity against cache metadata. Check persistent storage delete status before expiration window.
strategies.response: Confirm deletion if valid. Return error if storage is locked or read-only. Flag deletion request for manual review if purge fails within 24 hours.

strategies.detection: Verify backup checksums before replication. Monitor replication job status endpoint. Check storage connectivity via health probes.
strategies.response: Abort replication on checksum mismatch. Retry with exponential backoff on transient network failures. Alert on repeated integrity errors.

strategies.detection: Monitor stdout buffer memory usage. Verify ingestion pipeline status. Track log write latency.
strategies.response: Flush logs if buffer full. Alert on ingestion failure < 1 minute. Continue non-blocking writes to avoid request drop.

strategies.detection: Ingest events from platform-fault-tolerance. Monitor synthetic probe failures. Detect regional heartbeat loss.
strategies.response: Raise recovery-trigger event. Isolate failed region. Generate state-restoration event without crashing the orchestrator.

strategies.detection: Resolve region topology from repository. Check endpoint availability in DR storage. Validate state-restoration sequence plan integrity.
strategies.response: Generate state-restoration sequence. Fallback to standby endpoint if primary unreachable. Escalate to human judgment on complex topology conflicts.

strategies.detection: Track request duration vs SLA. Monitor connection lifecycle. Detect slow query patterns.
strategies.response: Terminate long-running request safely. Return 504 Timeout. Prevent service crash on timeout condition.

strategies.detection: Monitor signal listeners. Check drain window status. Validate resource cleanup hooks status.
strategies.response: Accept no new requests during drain. Terminate process gracefully. Fail if cleanup fails within allowed window.

strategies.detection: Read Prometheus histogram P95. Compare against 100 ms threshold. Check metric availability.
strategies.response: Alert on latency spike. Mark region degraded. Trigger scale-up logic if capacity allows.

strategies.detection: Execute synthetic probe. Check response time. Verify uptime threshold ≥ 99.9%.
strategies.response: Report downtime if threshold breached. Flag planned maintenance. Adjust uptime calculation for valid maintenance windows.

strategies.detection: Check WAF rules. Scan URL for threats. Validate input schema. Verify threat feed latency ≤ 5 seconds.
strategies.response: Block request if malicious. Return 400 for invalid schema. Return 429 if rate limit exceeded. Log blocked attempt.

strategies.detection: Check TLS certificate validity. Validate HSTS header presence. Verify SecureChannel origin.
strategies.response: Redirect HTTP requests to HTTPS. Inject HSTS headers. Terminate insecure connection.

baseline.thinking:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   **Role:** [JC:Technical Spec Agent].
    *   **Task:** Specify Error Handling Strategies for Sub-Phase 5.3.
    *   **Context:** TinyURL system (URL shortening, secure, minimal, public).
    *   **Components:** List of components provided (e.g., `comp-link-mapping-repository`, `comp-gdpr-exporter`, `comp-delete-purger`, etc.).
    *   **Constraints:**
        *   JSON output only (start with `{`, end with `}`).
        *   No markdown fences (` ```json `).
        *   Top-level key MUST be `strategies` (array).
        *   Do NOT wrap under `error_handling_strategies`.
        *   Error types must be from upstream docs/platform standards or generic platform errors (no invention). Use `__OPEN_QUESTION__` if silent.
        *   Detection/Response/Surfacing must be grounded/upstream-grounded (no invented regexes/tooling specifics unless upstream mentions them).
        *   Governed Stream constraints (lossless, single-threaded, human approval for gates, etc.) apply to the *system* behavior, but the output is the *specification* for error handling within the components.
        *   Authority 7 constraints (no truncation, full delivery, etc.) apply to the reasoning process (internal), but the output must be the JSON.
    *   **Input Data:** System Requirements (SR-001 to SR-017), Component Model, Backlog, API Definitions.

2.  **Analyze Components & Requirements:**
    *   I need to map each component in the `[Backlog]` list to specific error handling strategies.
    *   I need to derive error types from SRs and API definitions (standard HTTP codes, validation errors, security errors, etc.).
    *   *Constraint Check:* "Each entry in `error_types[]` MUST be one of: (a) concrete error name documented in upstream source... (b) generic platform-level error type... (c) `open_question` placeholder". I must *not* invent specific names like `EVENT_LEDGER_OVERFLOW`. I need to stick to standard HTTP status codes or generic categories (Validation, Authorization, Timeout, Internal, Network) unless the SR defines a specific named error. The SRs define *behavior* (e.g., "reject malicious", "block threat indicators"), which implies specific error types (e.g., `forbidden`, `bad_request`, `internal_server_error`).
    *   *Constraint Check:* "Detection, response, surfacing fields MUST describe upstream-grounded mechanisms". I cannot say "use regex X". I should say "validate schema" or "check threat feed".

3.  **Map Components to Strategies:**
    *   `comp-link-mapping-repository`: Handles POST /shorten, GET /redirect, GET /meta.
        *   Errors: Invalid slug (Validation), Slug exists/unique constraint (Validation/Conflict), Malicious URL (Validation), HTTPS failure (Transport), DB connection (Internal).
        *   SRs: SR-001 (mapping lookup), SR-002 (validate/reject), SR-006 (encrypt), SR-009 (unique).
    *   `comp-gdpr-exporter`: POST /gdpr/export.
        *   Errors: Unauthorized (Auth), No data found (Not Found), Export failure (Internal).
        *   SRs: SR-011 (GDPR Right to Access).
    *   `comp-delete-purger`: POST /admin/purge.
        *   Errors: Unauthorized (Auth), Invalid request (Validation), Storage purge failure (Internal).
        *   SRs: SR-010 (purge requests).
    *   `comp-backup-replication-engine`: POST /dr/backup, GET /dr/status.
        *   Errors: Integrity check failure (Internal), Replication timeout (Timeout), Auth failure (Auth).
        *   SRs: SR-012 (daily backups).
    *   `comp-platform-audit`: GET /audit/health.
        *   Errors: Ingestion failure (Internal), Storage failure (Internal).
        *   SRs: SR-007 (logs).
    *   `comp-failure-detection`: POST /fault/report.
        *   Errors: Event ingestion (Validation), Region failure (Internal).
        *   SRs: SR-005 (RTO).
    *   `comp-recovery-planner`: GET /recovery/plan.
        *   Errors: Unavailable region (Internal), Topology mismatch (Validation).
        *   SRs: SR-005, SR-006.
    *   `comp-request-timeout-enforcer`: GET /middleware/health.
        *   Errors: Timeout (Timeout), Slow query (Internal).
        *   SRs: SR-017 (timeout conditions).
    *   `comp-rel-latency-monitor`: GET /metrics/latency.
        *   Errors: High latency (Internal/Monitoring).
        *   SRs: SR-003 (latency P95).
    *   `comp-rel-uptime-verifier`: GET /health/uptime.
        *   Errors: Probe failure (Network), Service down (Internal).
        *   SRs: SR-004 (uptime).
    *   `comp-sec-request-validation`: GET /waf/status.
        *   Errors: Malicious request (Validation/Forbidden).
        *   SRs: SR-002, SR-013, SR-015.
    *   `comp-sec-transport-enforcer`: GET /transport/config.
        *   Errors: SSL failure (Transport), HSTS mismatch (Validation).
        *   SRs: SR-008.
    *   `comp-request-timeout-enforcer`: (Wait, is this the same as comp-failure-detection? No, looking at backlog list).
        *   Actually, the backlog list has `comp-request-timeout-enforcer` AND `comp-failure-detection`.
        *   I need to make sure I cover all components listed in `[Backlog]`.

    *   List of Components from Backlog:
        1.  comp-delete-purger
        2.  comp-gdpr-exporter
        3.  comp-link-mapping-repository
        4.  comp-platform-audit
        5.  comp-backup-replication-engine
        6.  comp-failure-detection
        7.  comp-recovery-planner
        8.  comp-request-timeout-enforcer
        9.  comp-graceful-shutdown-coordinator (Missing from API defs list in prompt, but listed in Backlog text? Wait, Backlog text lists `comp-graceful-shutdown-coordinator` under "Graceful Shutdown Coordinator" description but it's not explicitly in the "API Definitions" section. However, it is in the `[Backlog]` section text: `[Backlog] graceful-shutdown-coordinator (Tier D leaf under platform-fault-tolerance): Graceful Shutdown Coordinator`. I must include it.)
        10. comp-rel-latency-monitor
        11. comp-rel-uptime-verifier
        12. comp-sec-request-validation
        13. comp-sec-transport-enforcer

4.  **Drafting the JSON:**
    *   Root: `{ "strategies": [ ... ] }`
